message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first runtime.rs maintenance + ingest paths (tick 251)

First same-crate migration sweep of ambient storage calls inside
`frankenterm-core/runtime.rs`. Six call sites across three functional
clusters inside long-running spawned tasks:

**Retention maintenance loop** (L1524 area):
- `storage.purge_audit_actions_before(cutoff_ms)` →
  `storage.purge_audit_actions_before_with_cx(&purge_cx, cutoff_ms)`

**Pane-setup block** in capture loop (L2076-2118 area, shared binding):
- `storage.get_pane(pane_id)` → `get_pane_with_cx(&pane_setup_cx, pane_id)`
- `storage.upsert_pane(record)` → `upsert_pane_with_cx(&pane_setup_cx, record)`
- `storage.get_max_seq(pane_id)` → `get_max_seq_with_cx(&pane_setup_cx, pane_id)`

All three reuse a single `pane_setup_cx` binding declared above the block
(shared-cx pattern from tick 247).

**Event-record site** in detection loop (L2974 area):
- `storage.record_event(stored_event)` →
  `record_event_with_cx(&event_cx, stored_event)`

**Native-event pane handler** (L3121/3124 area, shared binding):
- `storage.upsert_pane(record)` → `upsert_pane_with_cx(&native_event_cx, record)`
- `storage.get_max_seq(pane_id)` → `get_max_seq_with_cx(&native_event_cx, pane_id)`

All six sites resolve cx via `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`.
These live inside spawned maintenance/capture tasks that don't thread a
Cx param through their fn signatures, so call-time resolution is the
correct pattern: each iteration picks up whatever Cx is active on the
worker thread (fresh per-iteration, not cached across runs of the loop).

This means retention purges, pane-setup writes, segment-event recording,
and native pane-event writes all become cancel-aware — an operator-
cancellable Cx at any point in the observation-runtime dispatch chain
now cleanly propagates into storage-layer mpsc backpressure routing.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 65 (187-251).
First runtime.rs same-crate sweep: 6 sites.
main.rs cross-crate total: 125 sites (unchanged).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
